mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances following a 0.2pp unemployment surge, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or combined Rank IC drops below 0.04 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects immediate supply-side capacity utilization and short-term economic momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during tightening cycles, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation combined with restrictive monetary policy creates stagflationary pressures that erode real corporate margins and compress equity multiples, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when both inflation and rates are elevated, or the interaction term loses explanatory power with partial R-squared below 0.01 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household disposable income and consumer spending capacity, supporting earnings growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive forward returns, or the signal generates negative alpha during periods of wage-price spirals with Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Sub-1% quarterly GDP growth indicates severe demand weakness and recessionary conditions, prompting equity deleveraging and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Markets price in the slowdown in advance, resulting in positive forward returns despite weak GDP, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions with KS test p-value above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial output and payroll employment confirm synchronized supply and labor market strength, boosting short-term market sentiment and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns when supply growth outpaces labor absorption, or the interaction effect vanishes in high-volatility regimes where VIX exceeds 25.
